description: Chapter 5 - Traditional problems with central potential. A guided structure for separation of variables, spherical harmonics, the Hydrogen atom and the three-dimensional harmonic oscillator.

topics.title: Central potentials: chapter roadmap
topics.note: Why spherical symmetry separates universal angular physics from the radial dynamics of each potential.

topics.title: Separation of variables in spherical coordinates
topics.note: The spherical Laplacian, radial and angular equations and the separation constants that become quantum numbers.

topics.title: Azimuthal and polar equations
topics.note: Periodic boundary conditions, the magnetic quantum number and the associated Legendre equation.

topics.title: Spherical harmonics
topics.note: Normalized angular eigenfunctions, allowed values of l and m, orthogonality and angular probability patterns.

topics.title: Hydrogen atom and effective potential
topics.note: Coulomb attraction, the centrifugal contribution and the radial equation for a one-electron atom.

topics.title: Hydrogen radial equation and Laguerre solutions
topics.note: Asymptotic behavior, associated Laguerre polynomials and the normalizable radial wave functions.

topics.title: Hydrogen spectrum and degeneracy
topics.note: Principal quantum number, Bohr energy scale, subshells and the n-squared degeneracy of the ideal atom.

topics.title: Hydrogen orbitals and radial probability
topics.note: Total wave functions, radial nodes, most probable radii and the physical meaning of orbital labels.

topics.title: Three-dimensional harmonic oscillator
topics.note: Cartesian and spherical descriptions, central confinement and the effective radial potential.

topics.title: 3D oscillator radial equation
topics.note: Boundary behavior, associated Laguerre functions and the allowed relation between radial and orbital labels.

topics.title: 3D oscillator spectrum and degeneracy
topics.note: Energy quantization, allowed l values and the equivalence between Cartesian and spherical state counting.

topics.title: 3D oscillator wave functions and probability
topics.note: Normalized radial states, nodes, turning points and how orbital angular momentum shifts probability outward.

topics.title: Hydrogen atom and the periodic table
topics.note: Shells, subshells, orbital capacity, Madelung ordering and the quantum structure behind the periodic table.

topics.title: Hydrogen expectation values
topics.note: Radial moments, Kramers-Pasternack relations and Feynman-Hellmann methods for extracting observables.

topics.title: Chapter synthesis: solving central potentials
topics.note: A comparative map of angular quantum numbers, radial equations, special functions, spectra and degeneracies.
